title: pittosporum coccineum
intro: Pittosporum coccineum, also known as the red leaf pittosporum or New Zealand tobacco tree, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive red-veined leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues and soothe skin irritations. In terms of health benefits, the plant has been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, including infections and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum coccineum are typically made from its leaves, which can be dried, infused in water, or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
